info.title: Seed Stocks Service - Continuous Analysis Pipeline
info.description: Pre-computed stock recommendations with volume intelligence. OpenAPI groups: **Settings (UI)** = editable config; **Observability** = metrics and learning transparency.

get.description:
Check whether a symbol has an open tracked position and its current rank.

Returns:
  - **position_status**: ``open_position`` | ``ranked`` | ``in_universe``
  - **open_position**: True if there is an active tracked position
  - **can_add**: True if no active position exists (eligible for new entry)
  - **rank / score**: from the current ranked_stocks list (None if unranked)

Self-learning: performance aggregated by score bin (0_30, 30_60, 60_80, 80_100).
Returns avg_return_pct, count, success_rate_pct per bin per trade_type per horizon.

schema.description: Days of data when no date range given

get.description:
Enhanced performance analysis with flexible grouping and filtering.

- **group_by=score_bin**: Performance by score bin
- **group_by=source_arm**: Performance by ARM (Chartink query strategy)
- **group_by=outcome_type**: Performance by outcome horizon
- **horizon_type=time**: Only time-based outcomes
- **horizon_type=event**: Only event-based outcomes

parameters.name: group_by

schema.description: Group results by: score_bin | source_arm | outcome_type
schema.title: Group By

parameters.description: Group results by: score_bin | source_arm | outcome_type

get.description:
Get pipeline health status.

get.description:
DB and pipeline observability for monitoring (e.g. from a web app).

schema.title: Response Get Observability Db V2 Observability Db Get

get.description:
Market regime scoring observability per scenario for web correlation.

Returns latest market_context snapshot and per-scenario:
- context_score_adjustment: scorer adjustment from market_context_scoring
- regime_favorability: multi-horizon favorability [0, 1.5]

schema.title: Response Get Regime Scoring Observability V2 Observability Regime Scoring Get

get.description:
Get top stock recommendations for a trade type.

**Cold start**: When no pre-computed rankings exist (e.g. first deploy, empty universe),
the API fetches symbols from the query service (ARMs), builds universe, runs scoring
(using last-close/candle data when market is closed), then returns recommendations.
If the query returns no symbols, empty list is returned (valid scenario).

**Fast path**: < 50ms when rankings exist (reads from pre-computed ranked_stocks).

**Trade Types**:
- `intraday_buy`: Breakout/momentum (hold until EOD)
- `intraday_sell`: Mean reversion/shorting (hold until EOD)
- `swing_buy`: Pullback in uptrend (2-10 days)
- `short_buy`: Short-term buy trades (1-5 days)
- `long_term`: Long-term trends (30-120 days)

**Response includes**:
- Entry price, stop loss, targets
- Risk/reward ratio
- Entry timing windows (for intraday)
- Reasoning and signals
- Technical snapshot

parameters.name: trade_type

schema.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term
schema.title: Trade Type

parameters.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term

parameters.name: min_score

schema.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.
schema.title: Min Score

parameters.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.

get.description: Core metrics: positions opened/closed, win rate, avg return (single def §1).
Per-scenario universe counts, scores, staleness.
Historical market context timeline with computed multi-day trends.
Per-ARM outcome stats with score_bin and market_regime breakdowns.
Current ARM weights from Thompson Sampling.
Daily P&L and win-rate aggregation for charting.
Single endpoint for top gainers, losers, and top-traded. Uses Kite batch quotes for current prices when market open.

Universal position endpoint — single source of truth for position reads.

**include**: `summary` (aggregate over full filter set), `list` (positions array), or both.
**limit=0** with `include=summary`: summary only, no positions.
**scenario** (or category): `all` | `paper_trade` | `learning`.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max items (0 = summary only when include=list)
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max items (0 = summary only when include=list)

get.description:
Run full historical performance analysis on stored outcomes.
Returns ARM performance, score bin analysis, indicator correlations,
market regime impact, and time-of-day patterns.
When from_date/to_date are provided, analysis is limited to that time window; otherwise uses last `days`.

get.description:
Single source for Investing page: capital deployed, P&L, allocation by trade type.
Use include=summary,timeline,risk,protection to get all sections in one call.
Calculate exact trading charges for a hypothetical round-trip trade.

**Purpose:** Full dump of trading + system settings (admin/debug).

**When to use:** Rarely — prefer /trading/form and /system/form for UI to avoid loading unused fields.

**How to use:** GET; no body. For production UI use section-specific GET/PUT below.

**Purpose:** Raw TradingConfig JSON (all sections).

**When to use:** When you need programmatic access to the full config object.

**How to use:** GET; compare with PUT /trading using the same keys for partial updates.

**When to use:** After user edits fields from GET /trading/form.

**How to use:** PUT JSON body: only keys to change. Returns updated config.

**When to use:** Programmatic read of system config.

**How to use:** GET; pair with PUT /system for partial updates.

put.description:

**When to use:** After editing system tab from GET /system/form.

**How to use:** PUT JSON with nested keys matching SystemSettings sections.

get.description: JSON Schema of SystemSettings for UI form generation.

**Purpose:** Primary UI payload for system tab: api_limits, observability thresholds, alerts, position_opener.

**When to use:** System Settings page load for system tab.

**How to use:** GET; use how_to_use + commonly_changed_first to prioritize form fields.

get.description:
Database statistics and data quality metrics for admin dashboard.

Shows table sizes, data distribution, staleness, and cleanup opportunities.

**Purpose:** List registered ARMs (query service scan definitions) with field explanations.

**When to use:** ARM management UI, audits, or choosing a parent when creating a child ARM.

**How to use:** Optional query params: scenario, status, include_inactive. Response includes field_info.

schema.title: Response List Arms Api V2 Arms Get

post.description:
**Purpose:** Persist a new ARM after verify-query succeeds.

**When to use:** UI “Save ARM” after validation; arm_name must be unique.

**How to use:** Body: CreateArmRequest. created_by set to ui.

schema.title: Response Create Arm Api V2 Arms Post

**Purpose:** Distinct scenarios and ARM counts for dropdowns.

**When to use:** Before filtering list_arms or when building scenario pickers in UI.

**How to use:** GET only; no params.

schema.title: Response List Scenarios Api V2 Arms Scenarios Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Full ARM record including query_string and child ARMs.

**When to use:** Editing an ARM, debugging scan output, or reviewing hierarchy.

**How to use:** Path: arm_name (uppercase).

schema.title: Response Get Arm Api V2 Arms  Arm Name  Get

put.description:
**Purpose:** Partial update of query, parent, regime, description, or status.

**When to use:** Promote probation→active, fix query after re-verification, or disable an ARM.

**How to use:** PUT with only fields to change; empty patch returns no_change.

schema.title: Response Update Arm Api V2 Arms  Arm Name  Put

post.description:
**Purpose:** Execute a Chartink query via query service; preview symbols (no DB write).

**When to use:** Before POST /api/v2/arms to validate a new or edited query_string.

**How to use:** Body: query_string, optional widget_id. Check success and sample_symbols.

get.description:
Recent ARM execution log, newest first.
field_info:
  executed_at       When execution started
  duration_ms       How long the Chartink call took
  records_returned  Stocks returned by the ARM query
  admitted_count    Stocks that passed gatekeeper into universe (may be null)
  status            success|failed|empty
  market_regime     Indian market regime at time of execution
  pipeline_run_id   Groups all ARMs executed in a single pipeline cycle

schema.title: Response Execution Timeline Api V2 Arms Observability Execution Timeline Get

get.description:
Per-ARM aggregated utilization for the last N days.
Shows: how often each ARM ran, how many stocks it found, and success rate.
Useful for spotting ARMs that consistently return 0 stocks or are failing.

schema.title: Response Utilization Api V2 Arms Observability Utilization Get

get.description:
Self-learning state per ARM.
Combines Thompson Sampling weights (from learning service) with position outcomes
(from DB) to show how each ARM is performing and evolving.

field_info:
  thompson_weight     Current selection probability (normalised). Higher = more likely to be selected.
  alpha / beta        Beta distribution params. alpha = weighted successes; beta = weighted failures.
  observations        alpha+beta = total learning observations (not raw trades).
  win_rate_pct        % of closed positions with return > 0.
  avg_return_pct      Average return across closed positions.
  is_probation        ARM is under re-evaluation (lower prior weight seeded).
  regime_weights      Per-regime thompson weights if available.

schema.title: Response Learning Observability Api V2 Arms Observability Learning Get

get.description:
All ARM executions for a single pipeline run.
pipeline_run_id is included in execution_timeline rows and groups a full cycle.

schema.title: Response Run Summary Api V2 Arms Observability Run  Pipeline Run Id  Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** In-process rolling latency (p50/p95/p99) per HTTP route for scaling decisions.

**When to use:** Suspect slow APIs or before load tests; compare with /performance/external.

**How to use:** GET; sorted by p95 descending. Populated by RequestIDMiddleware after traffic.

**Purpose:** Latency of outbound Chartink/Kite/Yahoo calls (@track_latency).

**When to use:** API is slow but /performance shows low — check here for dependency bottlenecks.

**How to use:** GET; keys like kite.get_batch_quotes, chartink.execute_query.

**Purpose:** Inventory of every mounted route (method, path, tags) for clients and audits.

**When to use:** Generating client SDKs or verifying a path exists after deploy.

**How to use:** GET; scan routes[] for method+path.

**Purpose:** List symbols seen in ARM universe with latest fundamentals snapshot.

**When to use:** Research UI, screening, or joining candidate data with recommendations.

**How to use:** Optional filters: status, sector, market_cap_category, limit.

schema.title: Response List Candidates Api V2 Candidates Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Observability: counts, status mix, stale fundamentals %, sector breadth.

**When to use:** Dashboards, alerts, or after deploy to verify weekly sync health.

**How to use:** GET; read field_explanations in response for metric definitions.

**Purpose:** One symbol: current row + weekly fundamentals_history (trend arrays).

**When to use:** Deep dive on a single name; chart PE/ROE/margin trends.

**How to use:** Path symbol; optional history_weeks (default 26).

schema.description: How many weeks of history to return

post.description:

**When to use:** After fixing Yahoo URL, or to refresh fundamentals before Sunday.

**How to use:** POST no body; returns counts inserted/updated.

tags.name: Settings (UI)
tags.description: Runtime configuration allowed from the System Settings UI: trading limits, system API limits, staleness thresholds, alerts, and position-opener cutoffs. Prefer GET …/form for field explanations, then PUT with partial JSON patches.

tags.name: Observability
tags.description: Read-only transparency: pipeline health, ARM runs, learning metrics, latency, registry stats, and candidate coverage. Use these to monitor and tune the system; they do not replace Settings for editable config.

tags.name: Registry — ARMs
tags.description: DB-backed ARM registry: list, verify query, create/update.

tags.name: Candidates
tags.description: Candidate stocks from ARM universe + fundamentals history.
